title: In A Wood
content:
Hush, �tis thy voice!
     No, but a bird upon the bough
     Romancing to its mate, but where art thou
     To bid my heart rejoice?

     �Tis thy hand, speak!
     No, but the branches striking in the wind
     Let loose a withered leaf that falls behind
     Blown to my cheek.

     Hush, thy footfall!
     No, �tis a streamlet hidden in the fern,
     Thus from dawn to dark I wait, I learn
     Sorrow is all.
meaning:
"In A Wood" is a quintessential example of Dora Sigerson Shorter's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
